prescribing_info.title: ZOSYN (piperacillin/tazobactam) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
ZOSYN (PIPERACILLIN AND TAZOBACTAM) - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Pfizer

MECHANISM: Piperacillin (extended-spectrum penicillin) + Tazobactam (beta-lactamase inhibitor)

SPECTRUM:
- Gram-positive: Streptococci, Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA)
- Gram-negative: E. coli, Klebsiella, Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Enterobacter, Proteus, Citrobacter
- Anaerobes: Bacteroides fragilis, Clostridium species
- NOT active against MRSA or ESBL-producers

INDICATIONS:
- Appendicitis/peritonitis
- Skin and skin structure infections
- Postpartum endometritis/PID
- Community-acquired pneumonia
- Nosocomial pneumonia

DOSAGE - ADULTS:

Standard Dosing:
- 3.375 g IV every 6 hours
- Infuse over 30 minutes

Pseudomonal Infections/Nosocomial Pneumonia:
- 4.5 g IV every 6 hours
- Infuse over 30 minutes

Extended Infusion (Preferred for severe infections):
- 3.375 g IV every 8 hours infused over 4 hours
- OR 4.5 g IV every 8 hours infused over 4 hours
- Improves pharmacodynamic target attainment

MAXIMUM DOSE:
- 18 g piperacillin / 2.25 g tazobactam per day
- 4.5 g IV every 6 hours = maximum

RENAL DOSING:
- CrCl >40: 3.375 g q6h (standard) or 4.5 g q6h (severe)
- CrCl 20-40: 2.25 g q6h
- CrCl <20: 2.25 g q8h
- Hemodialysis: 2.25 g q8h + 0.75 g after each dialysis
- CRRT: 2.25-3.375 g q6h or 4.5 g q8h depending on type

HEPATIC IMPAIRMENT:
- No dosage adjustment required
- Monitor for prolonged elimination

PEDIATRIC DOSING:
- 2-9 months: 80 mg/kg (piperacillin component) IV q8h
- 9 months and older (≤40 kg):
  - Appendicitis/peritonitis: 100 mg/kg (piperacillin) IV q8h, max 3.375 g/dose
  - Nosocomial pneumonia: 100 mg/kg q8h, max 4.5 g/dose
- >40 kg: Adult dosing

ADMINISTRATION:
- IV infusion only
- Reconstitute vials with compatible diluent
- Standard: 30-minute infusion
- Extended: 4-hour infusion
- Compatible with NS, D5W, LR
- Do NOT mix with aminoglycosides (inactivation)

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- History of serious hypersensitivity to piperacillin, tazobactam, penicillins, cephalosporins, or beta-lactamase inhibitors

WARNINGS:
- Anaphylactic reactions
- Clostridium difficile-associated diarrhea
- Skin reactions (SJS, TEN, DRESS)
- Hematologic abnormalities (leukopenia, neutropenia with prolonged use)
- Electrolyte abnormalities (hypokalemia, hypernatremia)
- Seizures (especially with renal impairment)
- Bleeding manifestations

DRUG INTERACTIONS:
- Aminoglycosides: Physical incompatibility, do not mix
- Probenecid: Increases piperacillin levels
- Methotrexate: Increased methotrexate toxicity
- Anticoagulants: Monitor for bleeding
- Vancomycin: Increased AKI risk

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Diarrhea (11%)
- Constipation (8%)
- Nausea (7%)
- Headache (8%)
- Insomnia (7%)
- Rash (4%)
- Fever (2%)

MONITORING:
- Periodic: Renal function, LFTs, CBC with differential
- Signs of superinfection
- Electrolytes (sodium content ~64 mEq per 4.5 g)

STABILITY:
- After reconstitution: Use within 24 hours at room temperature or 48 hours refrigerated
- Extended infusion: Use dedicated line or verify compatibility

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 2.25 g (piperacillin 2 g / tazobactam 0.25 g) vials
- 3.375 g (piperacillin 3 g / tazobactam 0.375 g) vials
- 4.5 g (piperacillin 4 g / tazobactam 0.5 g) vials
- Premixed frozen bags: 2.25 g, 3.375 g, 4.5 g in dextrose

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: NDA 050684
Label Revision: 2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=c0b9a8d7-e6f5-4g3h-2i1j-k0l9m8n7o6p5

prescribing_info.title: VANCOMYCIN Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
VANCOMYCIN HYDROCHLORIDE - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MECHANISM: Glycopeptide antibiotic; inhibits bacterial cell wall synthesis

SPECTRUM:
- MRSA (Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus)
- Coagulase-negative staphylococci
- Streptococci
- Enterococci (E. faecalis, E. faecium - some VRE resistant)
- Clostridium difficile (oral only)
- Corynebacterium species
- NO gram-negative coverage

INDICATIONS:
- Serious gram-positive infections when other agents cannot be used
- MRSA infections
- Endocarditis (with aminoglycoside for synergy if indicated)
- Meningitis (CNS infections)
- C. difficile colitis (oral form)

DOSAGE - IV (Adults):

Loading Dose (for serious infections):
- 25-30 mg/kg IV based on actual body weight
- Maximum initial dose: 2000-3000 mg
- Infuse at rate ≤10 mg/min (or over at least 2 hours)

Maintenance Dose:
- 15-20 mg/kg IV every 8-12 hours based on actual body weight
- Round to nearest 250 mg increment
- Typical: 1000-1500 mg every 8-12 hours

AUC-Guided Dosing (Preferred):
- Target AUC: 400-600 mg•h/L (for serious MRSA infections)
- Use Bayesian software or first-order PK calculations
- Obtain levels at steady-state (before 4th or 5th dose)

Trough-Based Dosing (Alternative):
- Serious infections: Target trough 15-20 mcg/mL
- Less serious: Target trough 10-15 mcg/mL
- Obtain trough 30 minutes before next dose

MAXIMUM DOSE:
- No absolute maximum; AUC-guided dosing preferred
- Doses >4-4.5 g/day may increase nephrotoxicity
- Consider AUC rather than trough for dosing decisions

RENAL DOSING:
- CrCl 50-90: 15-20 mg/kg q12h
- CrCl 30-49: 15-20 mg/kg q24h (or 10-15 mg/kg q12h)
- CrCl 10-29: 15 mg/kg initially, then 7.5-10 mg/kg q24-48h
- CrCl <10: 15 mg/kg loading, then 7.5-10 mg/kg q48-72h based on levels
- Hemodialysis: 15-25 mg/kg loading, then 500-1000 mg after each HD (level-guided)
- CRRT: 15-20 mg/kg q24-48h, level-guided

ORAL DOSAGE (C. difficile):
- Mild-moderate CDI: 125 mg PO four times daily x 10 days
- Severe/complicated CDI: 500 mg PO four times daily
- ± IV metronidazole for severe disease
- May use vancomycin enemas (500 mg in 100 mL) for ileus

PEDIATRIC DOSING (IV):
- 15 mg/kg IV every 6 hours
- Maximum: 60 mg/kg/day
- CNS infections may require higher doses
- Level-guided dosing recommended

ADMINISTRATION:
- IV infusion at rate ≤10 mg/min
- Minimum 1 hour for 1000 mg; 2 hours for 1500-2000 mg
- Dilute in NS or D5W (5 mg/mL concentration)
- Rapid infusion causes "Red Man Syndrome"

MONITORING:
- AUC-guided dosing preferred for serious MRSA
- Serum creatinine at baseline, every 2-3 days
- Vancomycin levels for AUC calculations
- CBC periodically (neutropenia with prolonged use)
- Audiometry for prolonged therapy

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Known hypersensitivity to vancomycin

WARNINGS:
- Nephrotoxicity: Risk increases with high AUC, concomitant nephrotoxins (especially piperacillin-tazobactam), prolonged therapy
- Ototoxicity: Rare, usually with very high levels
- Red Man Syndrome: Histamine release with rapid infusion; slow rate or premedicate with antihistamines
- Neutropenia: With prolonged therapy (>7 days)
- DRESS syndrome
- Thrombophlebitis: Use central line for prolonged therapy

DRUG INTERACTIONS:
- Piperacillin-tazobactam: Increased AKI risk
- Aminoglycosides: Additive nephrotoxicity
- Loop diuretics: Additive ototoxicity
- Anesthetics: Increased hypotension, histamine reactions

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 500 mg, 750 mg, 1 g, 1.5 g, 5 g, 10 g vials for injection
- 125 mg, 250 mg capsules (oral)
- 25 mg/mL, 50 mg/mL oral solution

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: Various ANDAs
Label Revision: 2024
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/search.cfm?labeltype=all&query=vancomycin

prescribing_info.title: PAXLOVID (nirmatrelvir/ritonavir) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
PAXLOVID (NIRMATRELVIR AND RITONAVIR) - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Pfizer

MECHANISM:
- Nirmatrelvir: SARS-CoV-2 main protease (Mpro) inhibitor
- Ritonavir: CYP3A4 inhibitor (pharmacokinetic booster)

INDICATION:
Treatment of mild-to-moderate COVID-19 in adults and pediatric patients (≥12 years, ≥40 kg) who are at high risk for progression to severe COVID-19, including hospitalization or death.

DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION:

Normal Renal Function (eGFR ≥60):
- Nirmatrelvir 300 mg (two 150 mg tablets) with ritonavir 100 mg (one 100 mg tablet)
- All three tablets taken together twice daily for 5 days

Moderate Renal Impairment (eGFR 30 to <60):
- Nirmatrelvir 150 mg (one 150 mg tablet) with ritonavir 100 mg (one 100 mg tablet)
- Both tablets taken together twice daily for 5 days

Severe Renal Impairment (eGFR <30):
- NOT recommended

MAXIMUM DOSE:
- Nirmatrelvir 300 mg + ritonavir 100 mg twice daily (normal renal function)
- Do not exceed 600 mg nirmatrelvir + 200 mg ritonavir per day

TIMING:
- Start within 5 days of symptom onset
- Complete 5-day course
- Can take with or without food
- Do NOT crush, chew, or break tablets

PEDIATRIC (≥12 years and ≥40 kg):
- Same as adult dosing
- Not established for <12 years or <40 kg

HEPATIC IMPAIRMENT:
- Mild (Child-Pugh A): No adjustment
- Moderate (Child-Pugh B): No adjustment
- Severe (Child-Pugh C): NOT recommended

CONTRAINDICATIONS:

Drugs where elevated levels may cause serious/life-threatening reactions:
- Alpha-1 adrenoreceptor antagonists: Alfuzosin
- Antianginals: Ranolazine
- Antiarrhythmics: Amiodarone, dronedarone, flecainide, propafenone, quinidine
- Antigout: Colchicine (in renal/hepatic impairment)
- Antipsychotics: Lurasidone, pimozide, clozapine
- Ergot derivatives: Dihydroergotamine, ergotamine, methylergonovine
- HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors: Lovastatin, simvastatin
- PDE5 inhibitors: Sildenafil (for PAH)
- Sedative/hypnotics: Triazolam, oral midazolam

Drugs that reduce Paxlovid efficacy (CYP3A inducers):
- Carbamazepine, phenobarbital, phenytoin
- Rifampin
- St. John's Wort

DRUG INTERACTIONS (CRITICAL):

Ritonavir is a potent CYP3A4 inhibitor. Many drug interactions require:
- Dose adjustment of concomitant medication
- Temporary discontinuation of concomitant medication
- Or avoiding Paxlovid

Common interactions requiring management:
- Tacrolimus, cyclosporine: Reduce doses, monitor levels
- Warfarin: Monitor INR
- Oral contraceptives: May reduce efficacy
- Statins: Avoid atorvastatin >20 mg, rosuvastatin >10 mg
- Calcium channel blockers: Reduce doses, monitor
- Immunosuppressants: Reduce doses significantly, monitor levels
- Direct oral anticoagulants: Avoid or adjust based on indication

WARNINGS:
- Drug interactions (extensive - see full prescribing information)
- Hepatotoxicity (especially with ritonavir)
- HIV-1 resistance (if used in uncontrolled/undiagnosed HIV)

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Dysgeusia (6%)
- Diarrhea (3%)
- Hypertension (1%)
- Myalgia (1%)

POST-TREATMENT "REBOUND":
- COVID symptom recurrence reported after completing treatment
- Usually mild; may not require additional treatment
- Not an indication for re-treatment in most cases

STORAGE:
- Store at room temperature 20-25°C (68-77°F)
- Use before expiration date

HOW SUPPLIED:
- Daily dose pack containing:
  - 4 nirmatrelvir 150 mg tablets
  - 2 ritonavir 100 mg tablets
- Full course = 5 daily dose packs

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: NDA 217188
Label Revision: 01/2024
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=b4a3c2d1-e0f9-8g7h-6i5j-k4l3m2n1o0p9
